message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="blue" summary="Waiting For implementation ready for review">
The Waiting For feature implementation (Step 8 / US-GTD-5) is complete and all pipeline checks pass. Here is a summary for review:

## Implementation Summary

### Domain Layer (`src/domain/waiting_for_item.rs`)
- `WaitingForId(Uuid)` — newtype wrapper
- `WaitingOnPerson` — non-empty, max 100 chars, trimmed; constructed via `WaitingOnPerson::parse()`
- `WaitingForItem` enum — `Active` / `Completed` variants (same state machine pattern as NextAction/Project)
- 15 unit tests covering construction, validation, state transitions

### Infrastructure Layer (`src/infrastructure/waiting_for_repository.rs`)
- `insert_waiting_for_item`, `find_active_waiting_for_items_by_user`, `find_waiting_for_item_by_id`
- `complete_waiting_for_item`, `delete_waiting_for_item`, `count_active_waiting_for_items`, `update_waiting_for_item`
- Migration: `waiting_for_items` table with id, user_id (FK), title, waiting_on, created_at, completed_at

### Service Layer (`src/services/waiting_for_service.rs`)
- `add_waiting_for_item`, `list_active_waiting_for_items`, `count_active_waiting_for_items`
- `complete_waiting_for_item`, `delete_waiting_for_item`, `update_waiting_for_item`
- Error types: `AddWaitingForError`, `CompleteWaitingForError`, `DeleteWaitingForError`, `UpdateWaitingForError`
- All service functions have tracing instrumentation

### Routes (`src/routes/waiting_for.rs`)
- GET /waiting-for — list active items
- POST /waiting-for — add new item (title + waiting_on person)
- POST /waiting-for/{id}/complete — mark as received
- POST /waiting-for/{id}/delete — delete item
- GET /waiting-for/{id}/edit — get inline edit form (HTMX)
- POST /waiting-for/{id}/edit — update item
- GET /waiting-for/{id} — get single item fragment (for cancel edit)
- Full HTMX support with HX-Trigger announcements

### Templates
- `waiting_for.html` — main page with two-field add form ("What are you waiting for?" + "Who or what?"), empty state, semantic list
- `waiting_for_item.html` — item partial with Edit link, Received button, Delete button, all with aria-labels
- `waiting_for_edit.html` — inline edit form with title + waiting_on inputs, Save/Cancel, visually hidden labels

### Dashboard Integration
- Dynamic waiting_for_count displayed on dashboard stat card

### CSS (`static/css/main.css`)
- ~150 lines of waiting-for component CSS in @layer components
- Follows existing patterns (similar to projects, next-actions)

### Focus Management JS (`static/js/waiting-for-focus.js`)
- WCAG 2.4.3 compliant focus management for HTMX swaps
- Handles delete/complete (focus next/prev sibling), edit mode, empty state transitions

### Tests
- 85 unit tests (15 new for WaitingForItem domain)
- 220 integration tests (30 new for waiting-for routes)
- 11 Playwright e2e tests (all pass, no regression)

### Glossary (`docs/glossary.md`)
- Updated with WaitingForItem, WaitingForId, WaitingOnPerson types
- Added waiting-for actions, error types, state machine diagram, error copy convention

### Pipeline Status
- `cargo fmt --check`: PASS
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`: PASS
- `cargo test`: PASS (305 total)
- `npx playwright test`: PASS (11 e2e)

Please review and provide feedback. I am ready to commit once we have consensus.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Now let me check the CSS for the waiting-for components more closely, particularly focus styles and contrast.
Now let me check the existing focus styles to see if there's a global `:focus-visible` pattern and whether the waiting-for buttons inherit it.
Good -- there's a global `:focus-visible` rule. Now I have a thorough picture. Let me also quickly check how the `<time>` element and datetime attribute are used elsewhere.
No `<time>` elements used anywhere yet. Let me also check the `date_added` format and whether a machine-readable ISO date is available in the view.
Now I have the complete picture. Let me also check the edit form's Cancel link — I raised this in my pre-implementation review.

content.text:
I see that the existing pattern for Cancel is `<a>` (in next_action_edit, context_edit, project_edit), but the older todo_item_edit uses a `<button>`. The waiting-for edit follows the established `<a>` pattern which is consistent — though I'd prefer `<button>`, the consistency matters more. This is a pre-existing pattern issue, not something to block on here.

Now I have everything I need. Let me send my detailed review.

input.content: Heydon here with my full accessibility review of the Waiting For implementation. ## BLOCKING Issues ### 1. Accessible names on buttons lack `waiting_on` disambiguation In `templates/waiting_for_item.html`, the aria-labels are: - `aria-label="Received: {{ item.title }}"` - `aria-label="Delete: {{ item.title }}"` - `aria-label="Edit: {{ item.title }}"` As I flagged in my pre-implementation review: if a user has two items with the same title but different `waiting_on` values (e.g., "Invoice" waiting on Alice vs. "Invoice" waiting on Bob), these buttons are indistinguishable to screen reader users. **Fix**: Include `waiting_on` in the aria-labels: - `aria-label="Received: {{ item.title }} — {{ item.waiting_on }}"` - `aria-label="Delete: {{ item.title }} — {{ item.waiting_on }}"` - `aria-label="Edit: {{ item.title }} — {{ item.waiting_on }}"` Same fix needed in `waiting_for_edit.html` for the Cancel link: - `aria-label="Cancel editing: {{ item.title }} — {{ item.waiting_on }}"` This is a WCAG 2.5.3 (Label in Name) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) issue. Buttons that perform different actions on different items MUST be distinguishable. ### 2. Date display lacks `` element In `templates/waiting_for_item.html` line 6: ```html {{ item.date_added }} ``` This should use the `` element with a machine-readable `datetime` attribute: ```html {{ item.date_added }} ``` This requires adding a `date_added_iso` field to `WaitingForView` (ISO 8601 format like `2026-02-12`) alongside the human-friendly `date_added`. The `` element is semantic HTML — it helps assistive technology and search engines parse dates correctly. This is not ARIA, it's native HTML semantics, so it's the right tool. I know no other template in the project uses `` yet, but this feature explicitly shows dates as a core part of each item's identity ("date added" is one of three required display fields per US-GTD-5). We should get it right here and set the pattern. ## Minor Issues ### 3. Edit form labels are visually hidden — consider consistency In `templates/waiting_for_edit.html`, the labels are `class="visually-hidden"`: ```html ``` This is technically accessible (screen readers will read the labels), but it differs from the add form which has visible labels. The existing edit patterns in next_action_edit and project_edit also use visually-hidden labels, so this is consistent with the codebase. Not blocking — just noting it for the record. ### 4. `waiting_on` display lacks semantic distinction for screen readers In the item partial, the `waiting_on` person is just a ``: ```html {{ item.waiting_on }} ``` A screen reader user hearing "Invoice Finance team" won't know that "Finance team" is the person/entity being waited on. Consider adding a visually hidden prefix: ```html Waiting on: {{ item.waiting_on }} ``` This gives screen reader users the context they need to parse the item structure. Minor because the page heading "Waiting For" provides some context, and the `waiting_on` appears in the aria-labels once the first BLOCKING issue is fixed. ## Nice-to-have ### 5. Cancel in edit form is `` not `

content.text: I've sent my detailed accessibility review to the Driver. I found two blocking issues and two minor/nice-to-have items. Now waiting for Luca to address the blocking issues before I can give consensus approval.
